era_context: The year before the Persian invasion of Greece. The Greek city-states are organizing their defense against the anticipated Persian attack. The Achaemenid Empire under Xerxes I is preparing the largest military expedition of the ancient world. In China, the Spring and Autumn Period continues under the Zhou Dynasty, with Confucius still alive and teaching. India sees the consolidation of early kingdoms and republics.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most of Asia beyond China and India

events.title: Greek alliance against Persia (Hellenic League formation)
events.description: In response to the threat of Persian invasion, the Greek city-states formed a military alliance known as the Hellenic League (or Hellenic Confederacy). Sparta, as the leading military power, assumed command. Athens, Corinth, Aegina, and other city-states joined the alliance. This unprecedented cooperation among traditionally rival Greek poleis was formalized at a congress, likely in late 481 BCE or early 480 BCE. The alliance established a unified command structure and coordinated defense strategy, which would prove crucial in the coming year's campaigns.
events.certainty_note: The formation of the alliance is attested in Greek sources, but the exact date (481 or 480 BCE) is uncertain. Herodotus places it before the invasion, but the precise timing is debated.

events.title: Persian preparations for the invasion of Greece
events.description: Xerxes I and his generals spent 481 BCE (and the preceding years) assembling the largest military force yet seen in the ancient world for the invasion of Greece. The preparations included the construction of supply lines, the gathering of troops from across the empire, and the building of a pontoon bridge across the Hellespont. Herodotus reports that Xerxes convened a council of his generals and nobles to discuss the invasion, though the historicity of specific details is debated. The scale of preparation was unprecedented and reflected the Persian Empire's vast resources.
events.certainty_note: The preparations for the invasion are well-attested. The scale and timeline are secure, though specific details vary between sources.

events.title: Confucius' final years of teaching
events.description: In 481 BCE, Confucius was in his final years of life (he was approximately 70 years old, born in 551 BCE). He continued to teach and refine his philosophical ideas about ethics, governance, and social relationships. His disciples were recording his sayings and teachings, which would later be compiled into the Analects (Lunyu). This period saw the consolidation of his philosophical system, which emphasized ritual propriety (li), filial piety (xiao), and virtuous governance (de). His influence on Chinese thought was already significant among his followers, though his broader impact would come after his death.

sources.name: Shiji (Records of the Grand Historian) by Sima Qian

events.certainty_note: Confucius' life dates and general activities are attested in later sources. The specific events of 481 BCE are not documented; this entry reflects the general context of his final years.

events.title: Magadha consolidation under Bimbisara
events.description: King Bimbisara of Magadha (r. c. 543–491 BCE) continued to consolidate power in the Gangetic plain during this period. Bimbisara is credited with expanding Magadha's territory through conquest and strategic alliances, establishing it as a major power in northern India. According to Buddhist texts, Bimbisara was a patron of the Buddha and supported the early Buddhist sangha. His reign marked the beginning of Magadha's rise to dominance, which would culminate in the Mauryan Empire.
events.certainty_note: Bimbisara's reign is attested in Buddhist and Jain texts, but these were compiled centuries later. The chronology is approximate within ±10–20 years.

disconfirming_evidence: The exact date of the Hellenic League's formation is debated; some scholars place it in 480 BCE rather than 481 BCE. The scale of Persian preparations reported by Herodotus (1 million+ troops) is almost certainly exaggerated; modern estimates suggest 100,000–300,000 troops. Confucius' exact activities in 481 BCE are not documented; the entry reflects general context rather than specific events.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 481 BCE is less rich than for 480 BCE because the major events (the invasion and battles) occurred in 480 BCE. Greek sources focus on the invasion preparations and the alliance formation, but dating is uncertain. Indian and Chinese sources are late compilations with limited contemporary verification. The year is primarily significant as a prelude to the major events of 480 BCE.

graph_edges.note: The formation of the Hellenic League in 481 BCE enabled the coordinated Greek defense at Thermopylae and Salamis in 480 BCE.
Persian military preparations in 481 BCE culminated in the invasion and battles of 480 BCE.
